description:
Your can test sensors in your smartphone.

Supported sensors:
- Accelerometer
- Light sensor
- Proximity sensor
- Magnetometer
- Gyroscope
- Barometer (pressure sensor)
- Compass 

If sensor registered in system,  it will be have green indicator,  otherwise it will be red.

If sensor don't report any data, it will be with label "no data" on sensor test screen. Than most situations it means that devices doesn't have it type of sensor, for other case it not working.

If all sensors don't report any data, it usually mean problem with communication sensors via sensor service. In most cases it happen after firmware update. Sensors doesn't work in all apps.

Showed total available sensors count. When press to it opened list of the sensors. You can test all of them with graph view.

Also useful for developers, who build custom kernels.


Details:

---------------

Accelerometer
- measures acceleration along three axes x, y, z; units measurement: m/s^2

When oriented along the axis, the normal value is equal to the gravitational acceleration (g = ~ 9.8 m/s^2).
With the horizontal position of the device, the values along the axes: z = ~ 9.8 m/s^2, x = 0, y = 0).

Practice:
Used to automatically change the orientation of the screen when you rotate the device, in games, etc.

Description of test:
Test football. When the device is tilted, the ball should move in the direction of inclination. Try to score the ball into the goal.

---------------

Light sensor
- measures illumination; units measurements: lux.

Practice:
Used to automatically adjust the brightness (auto brightness)

Description of test:
Test with lamp. When increasing the illumination, the glow around the lamp changes from white to bright yellow.
Move device to the light or, on the contrary, go into a dark room.
Approximate typical values: room - 150 lux, office - 300 lux, sunny day - 10,000 lux and above.

---------------

Proximity sensor
- measures the distance between the device and the object; units measurement: cm.
On many devices, available only two values are available: “far” and “close”.

Practice:
Used to turn off the screen when you call by phone.

Description of test:
Test with lamp. Close the sensor by hand, the light goes out, open - light up.

---------------

Magnetometer
- measures the magnetic field readings in three axes. The resulting value is calculated based on them; units measure: mT

Practice:
For programs like compass.

Description of test:
Scale with level, which showing the current value. Move device close to a metal object, the value should increase.

---------------

Gyroscope
- measures the speed of rotation of the device around the three axes x, y, z; units measurement: rad/s

Practice:
Used in various multimedia programs. For example, in a camera app to create panoramas.

Description of test:
Shows a graph of the speed of rotation along the x, y, z axes. When stationary, values tend to 0.

---------------

Barometer (pressure sensor)
- measures atmospheric pressure; units measuring: mbar or mm Hg. (switch in settings)

Description of test:
Scale with level, which showing the current value of pressure.

Normal atmospheric pressure:
100 kPa = 1000 mbar = ~ 750 mm Hg.

Hassle-Free!
Provide your ID card, personal account number, active mobile phone number, and fill out the form in the UKU app.

description:
Tunai Cakra is an intermediary platform focused on providing loan matching and recommendation services for users in Indonesia. We do not provide loans directly, but instead collaborate with various official financial institutions to help you find the right loan product quickly to meet your financial needs in various situations.

We are committed to providing a safe, transparent, and efficient service experience, always prioritizing your privacy.

Platform Advantages
- Fill in your data once and receive recommendations from various institutions
- Select legal financial partners to ensure a secure and regulated service
- Clearly display interest rates, terms, and total payments to avoid misunderstandings
- All information is transmitted encrypted and securely protected to ensure user privacy

Partnership Product Information (Provided by Partners)
✅ Loan Ceiling Range
Based on products from partner financial institutions, loan ceilings typically range from IDR 500,000 to IDR 10,000,000, suitable for various personal or business funding needs.

✅ Flexible Loan Terms
Loan products offered by partners have terms ranging from 91 to 180 days, allowing users to choose a plan that best suits their individual needs.
✅ Legal and Transparent Annual Interest Rates
All recommended products have an annual interest rate (APR) of no more than 36.5%, and all fees and repayment amounts are clearly displayed before application, protecting users' right to information and choice.

Loan Simulation Example (Platform Partner Recommendation)
If you apply for a loan of IDR 1,000,000 from a partner financial institution recommended by the platform, with a 120-day term and an APR of 36.5% (daily interest rate of 0.1%):
- Interest = IDR 1,000,000 × 0.1% × 120 = IDR 120,000
- Total Payment = IDR 1,120,000
- Monthly Installment = IDR 280,000 (for 4 installments)
The calculation above is for example purposes only; the actual amount is based on the information provided by the partner.

Application Requirements:
- Indonesian citizens aged 21 or over
- Valid official identification (KTP)
- A steady source of income or proof of repayment capacity
- Must grant permission to the platform to conduct credit assessments and identity verification

Disclaimer:
Tunai Cakra is a neutral loan information service platform, does not provide loans directly, and does not charge any fees to users. All loan services are provided by partner financial institutions, and the final decision regarding approval, disbursement, and contract terms is made by the partner.

Privacy Statement
Tunai Cakra complies with data protection regulations in Indonesia and related jurisdictions and is committed to protecting the security of your personal information. We only collect data necessary for loan matching and credit evaluation. All data is transmitted and stored using encrypted technology and will not be shared with third parties without your explicit permission.

Contact
- Address: Jl. Lapangan Bola, Kebon Jeruk Sub-district, Kebon Jeruk District, West Jakarta Administrative City, DKI Jakarta, 11530 Indonesia
- Email: info@digitalvisionsoftworks.com

description:
Weather, climate, air quality and earthquake information that occurred in Indonesia. This mobile application was officially released by the Meteorology, Climatology and Geophysics Agency (BMKG).

Features in the BMKG Info mobile application:

1. Weather Forecast
Provides 7 daily weather forecast information for all sub-districts in Indonesia

2. Earthquake
Provides information on the latest earthquakes M ≥ 5.0, felt earthquakes, and real-time earthquakes along with the distance of the earthquake location to your location

3. Climate
Presents some climate information in Indonesia, including:
- Days Without Rain
- Monthly Rain Forecast
- Monthly Rain Analysis

4. Air Quality
Presents air quality information in terms of Particulate Matter (PM2.5) concentrations in several cities in Indonesia

5. Maritime Weather
Presents maritime weather information (ocean wave height) in Indonesian waters

6. Aviation Weather
Presents actual weather information and the next 4 hours for airports in Indonesia

7. Impact Based Weather
Presents weather forecast information that takes into account potential impacts that will occur

8. Ultraviolet (UV) Light Index
Presents information regarding exposure to ultraviolet radiation related to human health

9. Forest and Land Fire Weather
Presents information regarding the potential for forest & land fires and hot spots (hotspots)

10. Event Weather
Presents weather information for certain events/events

11. Weather Early Warning
Provides early weather warning information in all Indonesian provinces

12. Radar Image
Presents radar imagery for Indonesian territory

13. Satellite Imagery
Presents satellite imagery for Indonesia

14. BMKG Press Release
Presents official press release information issued by BMKG

15. Crowdsourcing
Crowdsourcing feature for earthquake and weather information

16. Voice commands
Feature to search for information on weather forecasts, the latest earthquakes and air quality using voice commands

17. Notifications
Providing notifications for earthquakes, early weather warnings, and BMKG press releases

18. Bilingual
Available in dual language format, Indonesian and English

Deputy for Instrumentation, Calibration, Engineering and Communication Networks
Meteorology Climatology and Geophysics Agency
Tel: +62 21 4246321 ext. 1513
Fax: +62 21 4209103

Web: www.bmkg.go.id
